title: Objects, Scope, Linkage, Storage Duration, and Lifetime · Spiral Systems Review
summary: After a delay, a release reviewer reconstructs the object lifetime and address ledger contract from partial traces, compares two near-miss implementations, and retrieves prior bounds and lifetime rules before feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for object lifetime and address ledger after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish an object, its value, representation bytes, address, declared type, and current lifetime.
Map scope, linkage, and storage duration independently for block, file, static, thread, and allocated objects.
Identify precisely when each object lifetime begins and ends, including allocated storage and subobjects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Const Volatile Limits
steps.instruction:
review case — A review native-systems team handles memory case 3490. Produce bounded C evidence to validate object lifetime and generation evidence; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use const for mutation contracts and volatile only for its defined access semantics, never as ownership or synchronization.

Inspect the Const Volatile Limits trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Const Volatile Limits.
